text: UC Berkeley Off-Campus Rental Services https://och.berkeley.edu/ Off-campus rental housing listings and services for students, faculty, and staff. Use the site to search for housing and roommates and to see what's nearby. The office can be reached at (510) 643-6544 or homeinfo@berkeley.edu.

The office can be reached at (510) 643-6544 or homeinfo@berkeley.edu. The site also points users to landlord complaint reporting, office hours and location, ASUC housing tips, help center resources, and contact information for rental support.

Avoid Scams & Fraud https://och.berkeley.edu/avoid-scams-and-fraud Common scam red flags include rent offered below market, requests to wire money, requests to use another listing service or escrow account to transfer money, claims of affiliation with the site, dramatic landlord stories, and landlords who cannot or will not meet in person.

Avoid Scams & Fraud https://och.berkeley.edu/avoid-scams-and-fraud Common scam red flags include rent offered below market, requests to wire money, requests to use another listing service or escrow account to transfer money, claims of affiliation with the site, dramatic landlord stories, and landlords who cannot or will not meet in person. If you suspect a scam, report it immediately with as much detail as possible.

If you suspect a scam, report it immediately with as much detail as possible. The site recommends contacting the listing service, local police, the Internet Crime Complaint Center, and the Federal Trade Commission.

The site recommends contacting the listing service, local police, the Internet Crime Complaint Center, and the Federal Trade Commission. Roommate safety tips include limiting what you share online, meeting in public places, talking by phone before meeting, using privacy settings, trusting your instincts, and never giving out banking information.

Contact Cal Rentals https://och.berkeley.edu/resources/article/5422-contact-calrentals Berkeley Off-Campus Rental Services contact information and office details. The resource is meant for students looking for housing help, roommates, landlord complaint reporting, office hours, and support services.

The resource is meant for students looking for housing help, roommates, landlord complaint reporting, office hours, and support services. Primary contact details are provided on the site so students can reach the rental office directly when they need help with a listing or housing question.

Berkeley Rent Board home page https://rentboard.berkeleyca.gov/ Latest news, featured events, and tenant resources for Berkeley renters. The site highlights registration fees, petition forms, rent ceiling guidance, and shelter plus care notices. It also provides counseling, meetings, committees, records online, news, events, and contact information.

It also provides counseling, meetings, committees, records online, news, events, and contact information. The home page is a starting point for people looking for tenant-rights guidance, city housing services, and ways to connect with the rent board office.

Berkeley Rent Board registration page https://rentboard.berkeleyca.gov/rights-responsibilities/registration Information about registering rental units with the Berkeley Rent Board. This page explains registration rules and the process for keeping unit information current in the Rent Board system.

This page explains registration rules and the process for keeping unit information current in the Rent Board system. It is a reference point for landlords and renters who need to understand registration requirements and rent board compliance.

Berkeley Student Cooperative home page https://bsc.coop/ The Berkeley Student Cooperative is a 501c3 nonprofit housing cooperative founded in 1933. The site highlights membership information, the member portal, vacancies for spring and summer, our houses and apartments, rates and availability, and the co-op mission of providing low-cost, community-oriented housing for students.

The site highlights membership information, the member portal, vacancies for spring and summer, our houses and apartments, rates and availability, and the co-op mission of providing low-cost, community-oriented housing for students. It also points to jobs, social media, the policy site, workshift site, and manager resources.

BSC Our Houses & Apartments https://bsc.coop/housing/our-houses-apartments Important information about the Berkeley Student Cooperative housing options. Cloyne Court is only open to UC Berkeley students and is substance-free and academically themed. Many houses have shared bedrooms, shared kitchens, shared bathrooms, and common spaces. Room assignments are made at the unit level.
Room assignments are made at the unit level. Food is included at all houses during fall and spring and at some houses during summer. Workshift is 5 hours per week to help run the house.

Workshift is 5 hours per week to help run the house. The page also describes individual houses and apartments such as Afro House, Casa Zimbabwe, Euclid Hall, Hillegass Parker House, Hoyt Hall, Kidd Hall, Kingman Hall, Lothlorien, Sherman Hall, Stebbins, The Convent, Wilde House, Wolf House, Fenwick, Northside, and Rochdale.

The page also describes individual houses and apartments such as Afro House, Casa Zimbabwe, Euclid Hall, Hillegass Parker House, Hoyt Hall, Kidd Hall, Kingman Hall, Lothlorien, Sherman Hall, Stebbins, The Convent, Wilde House, Wolf House, Fenwick, Northside, and Rochdale. Afro House emphasizes Black culture, history, and community.

Afro House emphasizes Black culture, history, and community. Casa Zimbabwe is described as a student co-op with a long history and a strong community identity. Euclid Hall is smaller and cozy, with a high single-to-double room ratio.

Euclid Hall is smaller and cozy, with a high single-to-double room ratio. Hillegass Parker House is limited to graduate students and re-entry students age 25 or older. Hoyt Hall is women-identifying only in fall and spring and co-ed in summer.

Hoyt Hall is women-identifying only in fall and spring and co-ed in summer. Kidd Hall is one of the smallest houses, with a backyard, pool table, and TV. Kingman Hall sits in North Berkeley near the Rose Garden and is known for its historic character.

Kingman Hall sits in North Berkeley near the Rose Garden and is known for its historic character. Lothlorien serves vegetarian meals and emphasizes activism and art. Person of Color Theme House (Castro) focuses on low-income, first-generation, immigrant, and marginalized students of color.

Person of Color Theme House (Castro) focuses on low-income, first-generation, immigrant, and marginalized students of color. Ridge House is a historic Tudor-style house with hidden stairways and a garden. Sherman Hall is ADA accessible and has DSP singles. Stebbins is described as diverse, with residents from many backgrounds.

Stebbins is described as diverse, with residents from many backgrounds. The Convent serves graduate and re-entry students in a clean, quiet former convent. Wilde House welcomes LGBTQIA+ and queer students and aims to create an inclusive community.

Wilde House welcomes LGBTQIA+ and queer students and aims to create an inclusive community. Wolf House is a short walk from campus and balances social life with quiet study space. Fenwick and Rochdale are apartment-style co-op options, while Northside is quieter and aimed at graduate and professional students.

BSC Academic Year Rates https://bsc.coop/housing/academic-year-rates The 2026-2027 academic year contract runs from August 21, 2026 to May 15, 2027. For room and board houses, the cost is $4,986.00 per semester for Fall 2026 / Spring 2027.

For room and board houses, the cost is $4,986.00 per semester for Fall 2026 / Spring 2027. The cost includes food, utilities, cleaning supplies, toilet paper, furniture, a rich social environment, workshops, and other amenities. Hillegass Parker has larger single rooms at $5,895.00 per semester.

Hillegass Parker has larger single rooms at $5,895.00 per semester. The apartments do not include food service and have different rates depending on bedroom count and complex. The page also lists payment schedules, cancellation deadlines, per-month estimates, and notes about Winter Break and deferred payment.

The page also lists payment schedules, cancellation deadlines, per-month estimates, and notes about Winter Break and deferred payment. The payment schedule for Fall 2026 is split into three parts: one payment due with the contract, one on September 9, and one on October 7.

The payment schedule for Fall 2026 is split into three parts: one payment due with the contract, one on September 9, and one on October 7. Spring 2027 uses the same three-installment structure with payments due December 2, February 3, and March 3.

Spring 2027 uses the same three-installment structure with payments due December 2, February 3, and March 3. If members stay through winter break, they do not incur additional charges for that time. The cancellation deadline for the full academic year is on or before July 8, 2026.
The cancellation deadline for the full academic year is on or before July 8, 2026. A member who wants to cancel only the spring portion must do so on or before October 7, 2026.

A member who wants to cancel only the spring portion must do so on or before October 7, 2026. The page also explains that BSC does not send bills and that members are responsible for making sure payment reaches the office on time.

The page also explains that BSC does not send bills and that members are responsible for making sure payment reaches the office on time. The room and board houses include standard singles, deluxe singles, doubles, triples, and other room types depending on the house.

The room and board houses include standard singles, deluxe singles, doubles, triples, and other room types depending on the house. The apartments section covers studio, 1-bedroom, 2-bedroom, 3-bedroom, and 4-bedroom options with different totals and monthly estimates.

The apartments section covers studio, 1-bedroom, 2-bedroom, 3-bedroom, and 4-bedroom options with different totals and monthly estimates. Some utility costs are covered by the BSC, while some apartment residents may pay electricity or utility overages depending on the complex.

Apartments for Rent in Berkeley, CA https://www.apartmentlist.com/ca/berkeley Apartment List shows Berkeley rental listings, average rents, neighborhoods, FAQs, and renter-confidence survey results. The city page reports the average rent for a 1 bedroom apartment in Berkeley as $2,665+ per month, with studio, 2 bed, and 3+ bed averages also listed.

The city page reports the average rent for a 1 bedroom apartment in Berkeley as $2,665+ per month, with studio, 2 bed, and 3+ bed averages also listed. It highlights neighborhoods such as West Berkeley, Downtown Berkeley, and South Berkeley.

It highlights neighborhoods such as West Berkeley, Downtown Berkeley, and South Berkeley. The page includes FAQs about rent in Berkeley, cheap apartments, pet-friendly apartments, off-campus housing, a Berkeley rent report, and survey results about renter satisfaction.

The page includes FAQs about rent in Berkeley, cheap apartments, pet-friendly apartments, off-campus housing, a Berkeley rent report, and survey results about renter satisfaction. Apartment List's Berkeley rent report says the overall median rent stood at $2,125 in June 2026, with rent up 3.9 percent year over year.

Apartment List's Berkeley rent report says the overall median rent stood at $2,125 in June 2026, with rent up 3.9 percent year over year. The renter-confidence survey shows an A- overall satisfaction score, an A+ for public transit, an A for weather, an A- for jobs and career opportunities, a B for quality of schools, a C+ for safety and crime rate, a B- for commute time, and an F for affordability.

The renter-confidence survey shows an A- overall satisfaction score, an A+ for public transit, an A for weather, an A- for jobs and career opportunities, a B for quality of schools, a C+ for safety and crime rate, a B- for commute time, and an F for affordability. The city page also surfaces top neighborhoods and lets users jump to studio, 1 bedroom, 2 bedroom, and 3+ bedroom filters.

The city page also surfaces top neighborhoods and lets users jump to studio, 1 bedroom, 2 bedroom, and 3+ bedroom filters. It is a useful overview page for comparing Berkeley to nearby cities and for seeing where a student search might start before drilling into a specific neighborhood or building.

Apartments for Rent in Downtown Berkeley, Berkeley, CA https://www.apartmentlist.com/ca/berkeley/neighborhoods/downtown-berkeley Downtown Berkeley is described as urban, bustling, and walkable. Apartment List notes that academic energy thrives near UC Berkeley's campus, but it also warns about 2AM noise from Telegraph Avenue bars and limited parking during Berkeley Repertory Theatre events.
